title: ams-canon-code-auditor persona profile authored as first canon/personas/ entry in AMS
content: canon/personas/ams-canon-code-auditor.md committed at klappy/agent-messaging-service PR #79 (head sha c1b1af59). First persona profile in AMS canon; introduces canon/personas/ directory. Schema per klappy://canon/methods/persona-shaped-agent-runtime §The Persona Profile. Locked field values: persona=ams-canon-code-auditor; version=1; system_prompt_uri=ams://canon/constraints/canon-code-sync-via-spawned-agent-session; role=validator (per seven-value enum in klappy://canon/methods/spawned-agent-session-runtime-contract §Role; supersedes the older three-value sketch in persona-shape); mcp_servers.operational=[oddkit]; mcp_servers.task_relevant=[]; knowledge_bases=[klappy://, ams://]; surface_profiles.audit={density: medium, structured_output: required, output_schema: ams://canon/constraints/canon-code-sync-via-spawned-agent-session#how-the-audit-runs, max_emissions_per_session: 1}; brand_discipline=null. Three alternatives explicitly considered and rejected: GitHub at operational (rejected — task-relevant); single-KB ams:// only (rejected — cross-canon coherence drift surface needs klappy://); brand_discipline pointing at non-existent voice canon (rejected — pattern-coinage anti-pattern). Retraction conditions named: incompatible AMS-constraint amendment, schema additions requiring new fields, role-enum drop or rename. Substrate changes are operating notes in the AMS constraint, not retraction triggers. Doc is 1754 words, status=proposed, stability=draft, tier=2.

title: Section-level anchors are the canonical form when target content is a sub-bullet without its own heading
content: Handoff specified output_schema: ams://canon/constraints/canon-code-sync-via-spawned-agent-session#output-contract. Verification against the constraint source (12671 chars, fetched directly from main) found no #output-contract heading — the "Output contract." bullet lives inside ## How the Audit Runs and has no heading of its own. Resolution: anchor at #how-the-audit-runs (the existing section heading) and disambiguate inside the persona prose ("The contract is the 'Output contract' bullet within that section"). Alternative path considered and rejected: amend the AMS constraint to add ### Output Contract sub-heading — rejected because it expands canon-only PR scope and the constraint is semi_stable. The general rule: when oddkit_get section= is the consumer, anchors must target real headings; sub-bullet references are addressed at the parent section with prose disambiguation. Applies to any future canon doc that references mid-section content via URI anchor.

title: Gauntlet pre-PR runs cleanly when it's the standing checklist, not the post-mortem
content: Move D (audit-gate migration plan, PR #77) merged without oddkit_challenge, oddkit_encode, or oddkit_validate; operator caught the missing gauntlet post-merge and the retroactive trail landed as PR #78. Phase 1 of the same migration plan ran the gauntlet pre-PR: oddkit_time first-call, oddkit_catalog for canon currency check, oddkit_preflight before drafting, oddkit_challenge mode=canon-tier-2 surfaced two substantive gaps (confidence not signaled, alternatives not considered), doc edited to address (sections §Alternatives Considered + §Confidence added, ~290 words), oddkit_encode → this TSV before PR merge. block_until_addressed was false on the challenge so it was iteration not gating, but the response was substantive. Cost: ~one tool round-trip and ~290 words. Value: the doc landed stronger and the discipline trail is durable not retroactive. The Move D pattern (skip gauntlet, retroactive TSV) and the Phase 1 pattern (gauntlet pre-PR, integrated TSV) are both valid trails — Move D's retroactive shape is a fallback when discipline drift happens; Phase 1's integrated shape is the target.

title: The canon-code-sync audit gate auto-ran on PR #79 — the gate reviewing the file describing itself
content: On open, PR #79 triggered four checks including "Dispatch Managed Agent for canon-code sync audit." The audit gate's spawned agent session is reviewing canon/personas/ams-canon-code-auditor.md — the persona profile that describes the session shape the audit gate will eventually be dispatched as. Self-reference is structurally clean: the persona profile is downstream of the AMS constraint that already governs the gate, so coherence drift between them is the audit's job to check. If the audit passes, the gate has validated its own persona spec against existing canon. If it fails, the persona doc contradicts canon and needs revision before the gate can adopt it. Either outcome is informative. Recursion stops at one level: the persona profile itself does not introduce new canon that would require updating the audit gate's task scope; it instantiates schema already in place. The audit's verdict is pending as of this encode.

title: Phase 1 PR #79 open and gauntlet-traced; Phase 2 (audit-gate DO impl) is the next move
content: klappy/agent-messaging-service PR #79 opened at 2026-05-12T~03:08Z. Head sha c1b1af59. State: open, mergeable=True, mergeable_state=unstable (CI checks in_progress at open). Two files in the branch claude/persona-ams-canon-code-auditor-1c7b: canon/personas/ams-canon-code-auditor.md (the persona profile) and journal/2026-05-12-phase-1-persona-profile.tsv (this DOLCHEO trail). Hold for operator merge per standing PR-review-before-merge discipline. Phase 2 ready to start on operator signal: implement worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts as a Cloudflare Durable Object cribbing from worker/src/mcp.ts; wrangler.toml DO binding + migration; local-only test endpoint; gauntlet pre-PR. Estimated ~2-3 hours of agent-runtime cadence. Phase 1 persona profile is the resolvable URI the Phase 2 DO will look up at dispatch time (ams://canon/personas/ams-canon-code-auditor), so the canon must land before the impl is meaningful to test.
